query: I'm designing a multi-stage chatbot in my own code as a graph. What exactly is a node versus a transition, and what kinds of transitions are there?
expected_behavior: describe a node as a focused stage: an additive prompt + only its tools + its outgoing edges (and an optional tool-gate)
describe a transition/edge as target + type + label + condition/predicate, owned by the node
enumerate the three transition types: unconditional, llm (intent), expression (deterministic predicate over state)
note it is node-centric so a backward edge is just an edge whose target is an earlier node
notes: Guards the core data model and the three-transition-type vocabulary.
